methods.name: One Time Password Authentication (hardware-based)
methods.description: One-Time Password (OTP) authentication is a method in which a unique, single-use password is generated by an authenticator and transmitted to the user through a controlled delivery channel such as SMS, email, or a messaging service. The authenticator manages this transmission channel and sends the password directly to the user at the time of authentication. The user then proves their identity by returning the received password to the verifier. Security is based on the assumption that only the legitimate user has access to the controlled communication channel.
sources: @article{MayorgaYoo2025OTP,  author  = {Mayorga, O. E. A. and Yoo, S. G.},  title   = {One Time Password (OTP) Solution for Two Factor Authentication: A Practical Case Study},  journal = {Journal of Computer Science},  year    = {2025},  volume  = {21},  number  = {5},  pages   = {1099--1112},  doi     = {10.3844/jcssp.2025.1099.1112},  url     = {https://doi.org/10.3844/jcssp.2025.1099.1112}}
